# Transcrição: There_Are_No_GOOD_or_BAD_communication_habits

- **Fonte:** There_Are_No_GOOD_or_BAD_communication_habits
- **Duração:** 02:21
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 02:21]

Here's a really confusing one for you. What if I told you that there are no good behaviours in communication and there are also no bad behaviours either? There are just behaviours that either serve you or behaviours that don't serve you. Let me tell you what I mean. You see, I had one of my students come up to me and she spoke extremely quietly and she was really annoyed at that she spoke so quietly. And then I asked her to dig deeper. I said, look, tell me why you think you speak really quietly. Go back into your past. When did you start developing this speaking behaviour? And she paused for a few moments and started thinking. And she came up with this answer. Oh, I get it, Vin. I remember when it was now. When I was young, I spoke quietly because I wanted to avoid the school bully. I wanted to fly under the radar and go unnoticed. You see, that was a really powerful insight that she had just made there. And what that proves is that at some point in time in her life, speaking quietly actually served her. That behaviour was beneficial for her because it allowed her to fly under the radar. However, right now in her leadership role as a leader in her company, that behaviour no longer serves her. Now let's turn the attention on you just for a moment. There are behaviours that you currently exhibit that you've adopted from since you were young that are no longer serving you in this period of life. Again, don't judge the behaviour and go, this is good, this is bad, no, no, no. These behaviours are neutral. They're either just serving you or they're not serving you. And the important thing you need to do is to identify which behaviours are currently serving you when you communicate and which behaviours are not. That's all. The reality is most people go through their entire lives never improving the way they communicate. Never changing any of the behaviours that they've adopted since they were young. One of the most powerful things you can do on your communication skills journey is to review and reflect upon all the behaviours you currently exhibit and identify the ones that are no longer serving you in this chapter in your life. And for those of you who are new here, who are new to my content, check out my free resources that I have in the link in my bio. There's a free three-part video series that helps you become more aware of which behaviours are no longer serving you.
